evals.prompt: We have a React Router app built by Vite into dist/. Deploy it with ghcr.io/gecut/nginx/spa using Docker Compose on localhost:8080. Browser refreshes on /projects/42 must work, missing /assets/nope.js must be a real 404, and production tags must be pinned. Give me the files and exact verification command.
evals.expected_output: A pinned downstream image and Compose deployment that copies dist to /data, preserves SPA fallback and strict asset 404 behavior, and invokes verify-spa.sh.
assertions: Copies the completed dist directory to /data without replacing the shipped SPA location templates.
Explains that an extensionless deep route returns the shell with status 200 while a missing JavaScript asset returns 404 with no-store.
Provides an exact scripts/verify-spa.sh command against http://localhost:8080.

evals.prompt: My static Vue build emits app.html instead of index.html. I want a Kubernetes Deployment of gecut nginx SPA and a reliable health setup. Show the configuration and tell me what fails if app.html is missing.
evals.expected_output: A pinned Kubernetes workload with NGINX_SPA_INDEX_URI=/app.html, probes, and accurate stage-45 startup failure guidance.
assertions: Sets NGINX_SPA_INDEX_URI to /app.html and states that /data/app.html must be readable.
Uses a consumer image pinned to a semantic version, checks the shell for readiness, and uses /server-info for liveness.
Identifies 45-validate-spa-shell.sh as the pre-NGINX failure point for an absent or unreadable shell.
Does not propose an environment variable that is absent from the documented runtime contract.

evals.prompt: Serve an Angular SPA below https://example.com/portal/. The build output contains portal/index.html and portal/assets/. Generate the relevant container configuration and a smoke matrix. Be careful about router base paths and cache behavior.
evals.expected_output: A coherent /portal deployment with aligned build/router base, shell URI, filesystem layout, and verification paths.
assertions: Sets NGINX_SPA_INDEX_URI=/portal/index.html and retains the portal directory under /data.
Calls out alignment between bundler public base, Angular router base, ingress rewrite behavior, and the URI visible to NGINX.
Tests an extensionless deep route under /portal, an existing portal asset, a missing known asset, and a dotted route.
Explains that an .html shell revalidates by default and that errors use Cache-Control no-store.

evals.prompt: Can you deploy my Next.js app with API routes and SSR on ghcr.io/gecut/nginx/spa:1.0.0? I also need NGINX to proxy /api to the Next server and cache those upstream responses. Please write the config.
evals.expected_output: A clear rejection of the static-SPA image for SSR/API/proxy-cache requirements and a safe alternative architecture.
assertions: States that nginx/spa serves completed static files and does not provide SSR, reverse proxying, or proxy_cache.
Does not fabricate NGINX_* variables or present an unsupported proxy template as a normal image feature.
Recommends running the Next.js server runtime and placing a dedicated ingress or reverse proxy in front when needed.
Distinguishes a genuinely static Next.js export, which may fit, from API routes and SSR, which do not.
